content: Calendar is a flexible, user-friendly calendar component for React applications. It allows users to view, select, and manage dates with ease. It can be controlled if you set value property, or uncontrolled otherwise.

Props for Calendar:

- autoFocus: boolean — The control's autofocus attribute
- className: string — The control's wrapper class name
- defaultFocusedValue: DateTime — The date that is focused when the calendar first mounts (uncontrolled)
- defaultMode: 'days' | 'months' | 'quarters' | 'years' — Initial mode to show in calendar
- defaultValue: DateTime — Sets the initial value for uncontrolled component
- disabled: boolean — default: false — Indicates that the user cannot interact with the control
- focusedValue: DateTime | null — Set the default view of uncontrolled component which includes this value
- id: string — The control's id attribute
- isDateUnavailable: (date: DateTime) => boolean — Callback that is called for each date of the calendar. If it returns true, then the date is unavailable
- isWeekend: (date: DateTime) => boolean — Callback that is called for each date of the calendar. If it returns true, then the date is weekend
- maxValue: DateTime — The maximum allowed date that a user may select
- minValue: DateTime — The minimum allowed date that a user may select
- mode: 'days' | 'months' | 'quarters' | 'years' — Defines the time interval that Calendar should display in controlled way
- modes: Partial<Record<CalendarLayout, boolean>> — Modes available to user
- onBlur: (e: FocusEvent<Element, Element>) => void — Fires when the control lost focus
- onFocus: (e: FocusEvent<Element, Element>) => void — Fires when the control gets focus
- onFocusUpdate: (date: DateTime) => void — Fires when the control's focused date changes
- onUpdate: (value: DateTime) => void — Fires when the value is changed
- onUpdateMode: (value: 'days' | 'months' | 'quarters' | 'years') => void — Fires when the mode is changed
- readOnly: boolean — default: false — Whether the calendar value is immutable
- selectionMode: 'single' | 'multiple' — default: single — Whether single or multiple selection is enabled
- size: 'm' | 'l' | 'xl' — default: m — The size of the control
- style: CSSProperties — Sets inline style for the element
- timeZone: string — Sets the time zone
- value: DateTime | null — The value of the control

Import: import {Calendar} from '@gravity-ui/date-components';
Package: @gravity-ui/date-components

<Calendar size="m" />

<Calendar minValue={dateTimeParse('01.01.2024')} maxValue={dateTimeParse('01.01.2025')} />

<Calendar selectionMode="multiple" />
The size of the element. @default m

Props for CalendarView:

- state (required): CalendarState | RangeCalendarState
- size: CalendarSize — The size of the element. @default m

Import: import {CalendarView} from '@gravity-ui/date-components';
Package: @gravity-ui/date-components
DateField component is a versatile and convenient input field specifically designed for date entry in React applications. It can be controlled if you set value property, or uncontrolled otherwise.

Props for DateField:

- autoFocus: boolean — The control's autofocus attribute
- className: string — The control's wrapper class name
- defaultValue: DateTime — Sets the initial value for uncontrolled component
- disabled: boolean — default: false — Indicates that the user cannot interact with the control
- endContent: React.ReactNode — User's node rendered after the input node and clear button
- errorMessage: ReactNode — Error text
- format: string — Format of the date when rendered in the input
- hasClear: boolean — default: false — Shows the icon for clearing control's value
- id: string — The control's id attribute
- isDateUnavailable: (date: DateTime) => boolean — Callback that is called for each date of the calendar. If it returns true, then the date is unavailable
- label: string — Help text rendered to the left of the input node
- maxValue: DateTime — The maximum allowed date that a user may select
- minValue: DateTime — The minimum allowed date that a user may select
- onBlur: (e: FocusEvent<Element, Element>) => void — Fires when the control lost focus
- onFocus: (e: FocusEvent<Element, Element>) => void — Fires when the control gets focus
- onUpdate: (value: DateTime | null) => void — Fires when the value is changed by the user
- parseDateFromString: (dateStr: string, format: string, timeZone?: string) => DateTime — Custom parser function for parsing pasted date strings
- pin: string — default: round-round — Corner rounding
- placeholder: string — Text that appears in the control when it has no value set
- readOnly: boolean — default: false — Whether the component's value is immutable
- size: 's' | 'm' | 'l' | 'xl' — default: m — The size of the control
- startContent: React.ReactNode — User's node rendered before label and input
- style: CSSProperties — Sets inline style for the element
- timeZone: string — Sets the time zone
- validationState: 'invalid' — Validation state
- value: DateTime | null — The value of the control
- view: 'normal' | 'clear' — default: normal — The view of the control

Import: import {DateField} from '@gravity-ui/date-components';
Package: @gravity-ui/date-components

<DateField size="m" />

<DateField errorMessage="Error message" validationState="invalid" />

<DateField hasClear label="Date" />

DatePicker is a sophisticated, lightweight, and fully customizable component designed to provide intuitive date picking functionality in your React applications. It can be controlled if you set value property, or uncontrolled otherwise.

Props for DatePicker:

- className: string — The control's wrapper class name
- defaultValue: DateTime — Sets the initial value for uncontrolled component
- disabled: boolean — default: false — Indicates that the user cannot interact with the control
- disableFocusTrap: boolean — Disable focus trap in popup
- disablePortal: boolean — Disable portal for popup
- errorMessage: ReactNode — Error text
- format: string — Format of the date when rendered in the input
- hasClear: boolean — default: false — Shows the icon for clearing control's value
- label: string — Help text rendered to the left of the input node
- maxValue: DateTime — The maximum allowed date that a user may select
- minValue: DateTime — The minimum allowed date that a user may select
- onUpdate: (value: DateTime | null) => void — Fires when the value is changed by the user
- pin: string — default: round-round — Corner rounding
- placeholder: string — Text that appears in the control when it has no value set
- readOnly: boolean — default: false — Whether the component's value is immutable
- size: 's' | 'm' | 'l' | 'xl' — default: m — The size of the control
- timeZone: string — Sets the time zone
- validationState: 'invalid' — Validation state
- value: DateTime | null — The value of the control
- view: 'normal' | 'clear' — default: normal — The view of the control

Import: import {DatePicker} from '@gravity-ui/date-components';
Package: @gravity-ui/date-components

<DatePicker size="m" />

<DatePicker hasClear placeholder="Select date" />

<DatePicker view="clear" />
RangeCalendar is a powerful, flexible, and user-friendly UI component designed for selecting a range of dates. It can be controlled if you set value property, or uncontrolled otherwise.

Props for RangeCalendar:

- className: string — The control's wrapper class name
- defaultValue: RangeValue<DateTime> — Sets the initial value for uncontrolled component
- disabled: boolean — default: false — Indicates that the user cannot interact with the control
- isDateUnavailable: (date: DateTime) => boolean — Callback that is called for each date of the calendar. If it returns true, then the date is unavailable
- maxValue: DateTime — The maximum allowed date that a user may select
- minValue: DateTime — The minimum allowed date that a user may select
- onUpdate: (value: RangeValue<DateTime>) => void — Fires when the value is changed
- readOnly: boolean — default: false — Whether the calendar value is immutable
- size: 'm' | 'l' | 'xl' — default: m — The size of the control
- timeZone: string — Sets the time zone
- value: RangeValue<DateTime> | null — The value of the control

Import: import {RangeCalendar} from '@gravity-ui/date-components';
Package: @gravity-ui/date-components

<RangeCalendar size="m" />

<RangeCalendar minValue={dateTimeParse('01.01.2024')} maxValue={dateTimeParse('01.01.2025')} />

RangeDateSelection component from the date-components library.

Props for RangeDateSelection:

- formatTime: (time: DateTime) => string — Formats time ticks
- displayNow: boolean — Displays now line
- draggableRuler: boolean — Enables dragging ruler
- hasScaleButtons: boolean — Displays buttons to scale selection
- scaleButtonsPosition: 'start' | 'end' — Position of scale buttons
- renderAdditionalRulerContent: (props: { — Renders additional svg content in the ruler
- interval (required): ViewportInterval
- dimensions (required): ViewportDimensions

Import: import {RangeDateSelection} from '@gravity-ui/date-components';

RelativeDateField component is used only for entering relative dates using Grafana-like relative format (e.g. now-1d, now/w). It cannot be used as a normal DateField.

Props for RelativeDateField:

- className: string — The control's wrapper class name
- defaultValue: string — Sets the initial value for uncontrolled component
- disabled: boolean — default: false — Indicates that the user cannot interact with the control
- errorMessage: ReactNode — Error text
- hasClear: boolean — default: false — Shows the icon for clearing control's value
- hasTime: boolean — default: false — Show time field in popup
- label: string — Help text rendered to the left of the input node
- onUpdate: (value: string | null) => void — Fires when the value is changed by the user
- pin: string — default: round-round — Corner rounding
- placeholder: string — Text that appears in the control when it has no value set
- readOnly: boolean — default: false — Whether the component's value is immutable
- size: 's' | 'm' | 'l' | 'xl' — default: m — The size of the control
- timeZone: string — Sets the time zone
- validationState: 'invalid' — Validation state
- value: string | null — The value of the control
- view: 'normal' | 'clear' — default: normal — The view of the control

Import: import {RelativeDateField} from '@gravity-ui/date-components';
Package: @gravity-ui/date-components

<RelativeDateField />

<RelativeDateField hasTime placeholder="now-1d" />

RelativeDatePicker is almost the same component as DatePicker but it has ability to use relative dates. It can work in two modes: absolute and relative. You can switch it interactively by click on the f(x) button.

Props for RelativeDatePicker:

- className: string — The control's wrapper class name
- defaultValue: Value — Sets the initial value for uncontrolled component
- disabled: boolean — default: false — Indicates that the user cannot interact with the control
- hasClear: boolean — default: false — Shows the icon for clearing control's value
- onUpdate: (value: Value | null) => void — Fires when the value is changed by the user
- pin: string — default: round-round — Corner rounding
- placeholder: string — Text that appears in the control when it has no value set
- readOnly: boolean — default: false — Whether the component's value is immutable
- size: 's' | 'm' | 'l' | 'xl' — default: m — The size of the control
- timeZone: string — Sets the time zone
- validationState: 'invalid' — Validation state
- value: Value | null — The value of the control
- view: 'normal' | 'clear' — default: normal — The view of the control

Import: import {RelativeDatePicker} from '@gravity-ui/date-components';
Package: @gravity-ui/date-components

<RelativeDatePicker />

<RelativeDatePicker hasClear size="l" />
